topic: A student finds out their scholarship was meant for someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed a monotonous tune that seemed to vibrate directly against Elara’s temples. She stared blankly at the acceptance letter, the crisp paper blurring into meaningless shapes. Another late night, another stack of dense philosophy texts that offered no solace. Her shoulders ached with a dull, persistent ache, the kind that settled deep in the bone after days of relentless pressure. When she finally deciphered the fine print, a cold, sharp realization pierced the exhaustion. The name on the scholarship—it wasn't hers. It belonged to someone else entirely. A sudden, heavy slump pulled her chair back, the scrape of metal against linoleum sounding deafening in the quiet aisle. She just closed her eyes, letting the weight of the revelation settle, heavy and suffocating.

[story 2]

Liam dragged himself to the campus coffee shop, the lukewarm latte offering no warmth. His eyes felt gritty, heavy with fatigue, and the effort of simply existing seemed monumental. He traced the lines on the worn wooden table, his gaze drifting over the empty space across from him. The email, when he finally forced himself to read it, was a physical blow. The funding, the prestige, it was all misdirected. A strange hollowness expanded in his chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. He picked up his pen, the plastic feeling slick and foreign in his hand, and stared at the blank page, unable to summon the energy to even attempt to write a coherent thought about what he was supposed to do next.

[story 3]

Maya slumped against the cool brick wall of the dorm hallway, the late afternoon sun doing little to lift the oppressive cloud hanging over her. Every muscle in her neck felt tight, protesting the long hours spent studying. She reread the official notification for the third time, her eyes swimming with unshed moisture. The truth, when it finally clicked into place, was devastatingly simple: the award was allocated to a different applicant. A slow, shaky exhale escaped her lips, a sound devoid of any real energy. She didn't move to call anyone or confront anyone; instead, she simply lowered her head, letting the sheer exhaustion of the deception wash over her, leaving her immobile and deeply still.
